response.justification: The candidate is a versatile polyglot engineer with a current Staff title, genuine high-throughput distributed systems experience (microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), and directly relevant AI-native exposure building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling at re:cinq. However, Python — a must-have — is absent from both his skills list and every role described, his deepest systems work was in the niche DLang ecosystem and ended in 2020, and the resume shows little evidence of staff-scope responsibilities like directing technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder coordination, or operational on-call ownership at scale.
strengths: Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling experience, matching the AI-native engineering baseline
Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems work (millions of HTTP requests/second) with async I/O and caching
Broad full-stack craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure
concerns: No Python anywhere on the resume, a stated must-have for the role
Most senior systems experience is in DLang and dates to 2018-2020; recent roles are product-focused full-stack work
Limited demonstrated evidence of staff-level technical strategy, stakeholder management, or on-call/incident-response ownership
